data.body: DONE (config-loop, commit 3656764a). Back-compat for the legacy flat .rebar/config.conf. AC1 (legacy still parsed + aliases honored): legacy dotted-key parsing (_read_legacy_conf) and the verify.require_verdict_for_close -> require_signature_for_close alias (_ALIASES, resolved per-layer in coerce_sparse) were already in place from 43a0/252e; LOCKED IN with parity tests — the SAME settings via legacy conf vs rebar.toml resolve to an IDENTICAL typed Config; comment/quoted-value/spaces-around-equals/keyless-line handling verified. Confirmed the 252e review property: aliases resolve per-layer to canonical BEFORE merge, so a legacy key in a lower layer + canonical in a higher layer compares by canonical name (higher wins) — test added. AC2 (unknown-key WARN during window, hard-error after, CONFIGURABLE cutover): added a strict flag threaded through coerce_sparse / Config.from_mapping / _ordered_layers; default WARN (deprecation window, unchanged), and REBAR_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEYS=error makes unknown sections/keys raise ConfigError. Strict is ORTHOGONAL to value validation (invalid values always fail-closed regardless) and to aliasing (a recognized legacy key still WARNs + is accepted under strict, never errors). Added the env var to the load_config cache signature so toggling the policy invalidates cached resolutions. Also made rebar config report ConfigError as a clean stderr message + exit 1 (no traceback). AC3 (tests): 12 back-compat tests + 1 CLI-error test. Subagent review: NO critical/major; verify-gate fail-closed preserved, alias-before-unknown-check confirmed, cache key sufficient. Full offline suite green: 1912 passed.
